title: Successful industrial policy must account for political economy
summary_en: Plain-language synthesis of the new industrial-policy research for IMF Finance & Development. Argues that industrial policy is back on the global agenda — IRA, EU Green Deal, China's Made-in-China 2025 — and that successful design depends less on the choice of instruments and more on the political economy of the implementing state: capacity, accountability, and resistance to capture.
policy_relevance: Reading-list item for any Hungarian ministry contemplating sectoral subsidies (battery, automotive, semiconductor). The framework's central warning — that capture risk dominates instrument choice — is directly relevant to the political economy of Hungarian industrial policy. Caveat: high-level synthesis, not a country case.
